arts.ttl: Mikel Merino rescues Spain as Croatia stun France in Nations League quarter-finals
arts.sum: Germany came back to beat Italy while Denmark shocked Portugal in the other two ties

arts.ttl: Netherlands 2-2 Spain: Highlights, man of the match, stats as La Roja fight back to leave quarter-final delicately poised
arts.sum: Mikel Merino comes up with a last-gasp leveller as Spain fight back to draw 2-2 with 10-man Netherlands in the first leg of their UEFA Nations League quarter-final.

arts.ttl: Spain open 2025 with Dutch draw in Nations League battle
arts.sum: Spain have kicked off 2025 with a 2-2 draw on the road at Ronald Koeman Netherlands in the UEFA Nations League quarter finals. Luis de la Fuente’s side finished top of their group at the end of 2024 and were drawn against La Oranje in the last eight. La Roja are battling to reach the […] The post Spain open 2025 with Dutch draw in Nations League battle appeared first on Football España.

Football-Oranje’s Michael, Mike and Abdul react to the Nations League quarter-final first leg between the Netherlands and Spain. Join in the conversation on YouTube! arts.ttl: Nations League: 10-man Netherlands draw with Spain
arts.sum: The Netherlands conceded a late equaliser as the first leg of their Nations League quarter-final finished 2-2. Ronald Koeman decided to start with Justin Kluivert as the ten, Jeremie Frimpong on the wing and Jorrel Hato as left-back for the clash with Spain. The European Champions were ahead early on as Yamine Lamal robbed Hato […] The post Nations League: 10-man Netherlands draw with Spain appeared first on Football Oranje.

arts.ttl: Xabi Alonso Named the 'Underrated' Englishman Who Could've Played for Spain in 2010
arts.sum: Xabi Alonso once named the 'underrated' England player he believed was good enough to play for Spain.

arts.ttl: Lamine Yamal impostor fools excited fan while casually walking down the street of Barcelona
arts.sum: A viral video making the rounds on social media shows a fan excitedly approaching what he believed to be Lamine Yamal, the rising teenage sensation of FC Barcelona, only to realize later that he had been completely fooled by an impostor. The lookalike, casually strolling through the streets of Barcelona with sunglasses on, played his role perfectly, making the unsuspecting fan believe he had just met the young football star.

arts.ttl: Liverpool 'identify Virgil van Dijk replacement' as Reds prepare to 'battle' Real Madrid for teenage superstar
arts.sum: Liverpool are allegedly battling Real Madrid for the signature of in-demand Bournemouth centre-back Dean Huijsen.

arts.ttl: Liverpool 'close in' on HUGE first summer transfer as double deal for Van Dijk replacement lined up
arts.sum: Liverpool are aware they may have a lot of work to do in the transfer market to build on this campaign with a number of first team players on course to leave this summer, including the out-of-contract trio Trent Alexander-Arnold, Mohamed Salah and Virgil van Dijk. Liverpool 'close in' on HUGE first summer transfer as double deal for Van Dijk replacement lined up

arts.ttl: Netherlands v Spain, Italy v Germany, Greece v Scotland, and more: Nations League – live
arts.sum: Nations League updates from 7.45pm GMT kick-offs Live scoreboard | Read Football Daily | And mail Scott Steve Clarke talks to BBC Scotland: “Come into the stadium and you’re buzzing already … was tempted to go to a back five but decided to stick with the four … the four over the group stage worked well … we created a little bit more … more midfield players on the pitch … it is a very quick start on a Thursday … the only time I had all the players on the pitch was on Wednesday just before we flew to Greece so we didn’t have a lot of time to change the system … so we stuck with what we worked on for the previous six games.” Steve Clarke sticks to four at the back … by the looks of it, given how the returning Kieran Tierney is on the bench. There are just two changes to the team that started the dramatic 2-1 win in Poland last November: Lewis Ferguson and Che Adams step up with Ben Doak and Lyndon Dykes missing out altogether.
